specific_location: west end of Greenhorn Meadows Park along Greenhorn Creek
description: This bird appeared smaller than a House Wren. It was somewhat pot-bellied with a very short thin tail upturned and almost perpendicular to its body. The overall color of the bird was a very dark rufous with little contrast of upper and underparts making ID fairly easy. Throat was a rich orange buff slightly lighter than rest of dark rufous underparts. Undertail coverts were darkly barred. Bill was dark & slim.  I saw no white spotting on blackish rufous wings, nor barring on belly. The bird seemed darker rufous than most Pacific Wrens I've seen, especially on the underparts.Behaviors: Darted about thick riparian habitat staying low to the ground, occasionally popping up on a twig for good views.Call: A fairly loud & somewhat burry repeated "jimp, jimp."Plumage: adult
